jokes.text: The local Bosnians are calling it a stunning re-invigoration of the 'Crows Have Eyes' franchise. That, of course, is a loose translation.
jokes.setup: Moira dramatically announcing her film success
jokes.punchline: Deflating the praise by admitting it's a loose translation from Bosnian
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - grandiose announcement immediately undermined by her own honesty. Perfect character voice.

jokes.text: Yes! Noon Bosnian Mountain Time, that is! Which was several hours ago, John.
jokes.setup: John pointing out she said noon, it's 3:30am
jokes.punchline: Moira's convoluted explanation about Bosnian time zones
jokes.explanation: Moira's refusal to admit a simple mistake, turning it into elaborate geography

jokes.text: I took a Bosnian upper just to keep awake. Those things are the size of a nickel, so I only took one, at least, I think I did. I may have taken a second one just to be safe, because I don't know if I took that first one, or not.
jokes.setup: Moira explaining her erratic behavior from the trip
jokes.punchline: The circular logic of taking pills because she can't remember taking pills
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating spiral of drug-addled logic, perfectly delivered

jokes.text: Either way, I've been up since eight am three days ago, with time changes factored in
jokes.setup: Moira's manic energy from the pills
jokes.punchline: The impossibly specific yet vague time calculation
jokes.explanation: Moira's mathematical precision applied to chaos

jokes.text: I don't know what you kids are still doing up so late.
jokes.setup: Moira waking everyone up at 3:30am
jokes.punchline: Her complete lack of self-awareness about who's keeping whom awake
jokes.explanation: Perfect character blindness - Moira genuinely confused by the situation she created

jokes.text: Ooh, frisky, John.
jokes.setup: John suggesting they go to bed to sleep
jokes.punchline: Moira's sexual misinterpretation
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection but effective character moment

jokes.text: Oh! Bye! / Ewww! / Eww. / Ugh!
jokes.setup: Moira's sexual comment to their father
jokes.punchline: The kids' horrified reactions
jokes.explanation: Standard gross-out parent humor but well-executed

jokes.text: You know, best to round up to an even four days.
jokes.setup: Moira explaining why she didn't sleep
jokes.punchline: Treating sleep deprivation like a math problem to be optimized
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Moira logic - treating exhaustion as an achievement to be maximized

jokes.text: Before your father met me, he was not quite so selective. I hesitate to add, he even had a predilection for boom boom girls!
jokes.setup: Moira finding love letters to John
jokes.punchline: Her euphemistic way of saying 'prostitutes'
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - using elaborate vocabulary to discuss crude topics. 'Boom boom girls' is perfect character voice.

jokes.text: Okay, I would like to remind you that I don't have a therapist anymore.
jokes.setup: Moira forcing Alexis to read explicit love letters
jokes.punchline: Alexis's practical concern about her mental health resources
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Alexis's deadpan self-awareness about needing therapy for family trauma is perfectly delivered

jokes.text: No, you've said that like, three times now.
jokes.setup: Moira repeatedly saying there's a logical explanation
jokes.punchline: Alexis calling out the repetition
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - Alexis being the voice of reason

jokes.text: Just a few more, just to lull me to sleep. That's all.
jokes.setup: Moira agreeing to stop reading the letters
jokes.punchline: Immediately making excuse to keep reading
jokes.explanation: Classic addictive behavior - making excuse to continue despite agreeing to stop.

jokes.text: Um, our money isn't for sale.
jokes.setup: Robber demanding money from the register
jokes.punchline: David's literal interpretation of the demand
jokes.explanation: Perfect David response - overthinking a robbery demand with retail logic

jokes.text: Yeah, I'd like you to open your cash register, and give me your money!
jokes.setup: Standard robbery demand
jokes.punchline: Sets up the entire robbery sequence
jokes.explanation: Setup for sequence but not funny on its own.

jokes.text: Our till doesn't currently have any cash in it, because my partner is at the bank getting money for the float.
jokes.setup: Being robbed at gunpoint
jokes.punchline: David explaining store operations like it's a normal customer service issue
jokes.explanation: David's customer service instincts overriding survival instincts - great character work

jokes.text: A wallet full of debt.
jokes.setup: Robber asking what David has
jokes.punchline: David's honest assessment of his financial situation
jokes.explanation: Economical and character-specific - David's financial reality delivered perfectly

jokes.text: Um, we only have cloth totes, I hope that's okay.
jokes.setup: Robber demanding something be put in a bag
jokes.punchline: David apologizing for not having the right bag type for a robbery
jokes.explanation: Exceptional character moment - David's boutique sensibilities completely overriding the danger of the situation

jokes.text: Do you drink red wine, or white wine? Maybe some chilled rose in the back?
jokes.setup: Robber accepting wine
jokes.punchline: David turning into a sommelier during a robbery
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of David's character flaw - he literally cannot stop providing good customer service even during a crime

jokes.text: Suddenly she's a sales associate.
jokes.setup: Stevie suggesting cheese to the robber
jokes.punchline: David criticizing Stevie for doing exactly what he's been doing
jokes.explanation: David's complete lack of self-awareness about his own behavior

jokes.text: Do you have bad skin? / Yeah, sure, just hurry it up!
jokes.setup: Offering skincare products
jokes.punchline: Robber just agreeing to speed things up
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the sales consultation during robbery.

jokes.text: God forbid he be specific.
jokes.setup: Robber accepting cheese but not specifying type
jokes.punchline: David being genuinely annoyed at the robber's lack of specificity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work - David's retail perfectionism making him frustrated with a criminal

jokes.text: Could you help the man with the door? His hands are full.
jokes.setup: Robber leaving with bags of stolen goods
jokes.punchline: David's polite customer service extending to helping the robber leave
jokes.explanation: Peak David Rose - his compulsive politeness overriding all common sense. Perfectly crafted character moment.

jokes.text: Could you help the man with the door? His hands are full.
jokes.setup: Robber leaving with stolen goods
jokes.punchline: David offering customer service to the end
jokes.explanation: Perfect capstone to the sequence. Peak David character work - customer service even for criminals.

jokes.text: Why do I feel like we did something wrong?
jokes.setup: The absurdly polite robbery just concluded
jokes.punchline: David's confused guilt about providing good customer service
jokes.explanation: Perfect capper to the robbery sequence - David's moral confusion

jokes.text: Tell that to me at 21, escaping the yakuza.
jokes.setup: Ted saying she can't run in her shoes
jokes.punchline: Alexis's casual reference to running from Japanese organized crime
jokes.explanation: Classic Alexis backstory drop - casually mentioning running from yakuza like it's normal life experience

jokes.text: Also, I forgot.
jokes.setup: Alexis giving an elaborate excuse about trauma
jokes.punchline: The simple, honest reason she didn't come
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis - deflating her own dramatic excuse with simple honesty

jokes.text: Okay, I asked if I should be worried.
jokes.setup: Ted saying he kept a waterproof watch from an ex
jokes.punchline: Alexis's practical response to his romantic gesture
jokes.explanation: Alexis cutting through romantic nonsense with practical concern

jokes.text: Like... that empty book that you gave me.
jokes.setup: Ted trying to think of meaningful gifts he's kept
jokes.punchline: Calling a journal an 'empty book'
jokes.explanation: Ted's awkward, literal description revealing he never used the journal

jokes.text: I'm just so scared of losing it.
jokes.setup: Ted noting he's never seen her wear the locket
jokes.punchline: Alexis's excuse for never wearing it
jokes.explanation: Weak excuse that sets up the later revelation she gave it away

jokes.text: No, but um, one time I did do a book report on David's diary, and it was very dark.
jokes.setup: Ted asking if she's ever written in a journal
jokes.punchline: Alexis casually revealing she read and analyzed David's private diary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis - casual violation of privacy presented as academic achievement

jokes.text: Although now that I'm standing here, it's equally possible that I didn't knock at all. I suppose we'll never know, will we?
jokes.setup: Moira claiming she knocked on Jocelyn's door
jokes.punchline: Immediately admitting she might not have and treating it as an unsolvable mystery
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - turning basic uncertainty into philosophical mystery

jokes.text: I haven't slept in eight months. / Why's that? / Because of the baby.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn claiming exhaustion like Moira
jokes.punchline: The obvious reason Moira didn't consider
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - Moira's self-absorption missing obvious realities

jokes.text: I suppose we'll never know, will we?
jokes.setup: Admitting she might not have knocked
jokes.punchline: Treating easily verifiable fact as mysterious
jokes.explanation: Good Moira moment - making simple thing sound profound and unknowable.

jokes.text: In Bosni-wood, as the industry will soon be calling it
jokes.setup: Moira talking about the Bosnian film industry
jokes.punchline: Her confident prediction about a nickname that will never catch on
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira delusion - creating Hollywood portmanteau for Bosnian film industry

jokes.text: I would be feeling it too, if I knew... what the heck you were singing about.
jokes.setup: Moira singing emotional Bosnian love song
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn's honest reaction to not understanding the language
jokes.explanation: Jocelyn cutting through Moira's pretension with practical honesty

jokes.text: I once found a bag of Roland's ex-girlfriends' bras. Burned them. Except for the ones that were my size.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn relating to Moira's jealousy about the letters
jokes.punchline: The practical exception to her dramatic gesture
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jocelyn - combining jealous destruction with practical thriftiness

jokes.text: It's like 50 Shades of Johnny Rose!
jokes.setup: Jocelyn reading the explicit love letters
jokes.punchline: Pop culture reference applied to Johnny
jokes.explanation: Predictable pop culture reference but fits Jocelyn's character

jokes.text: I can't believe you just offered him the wine.
jokes.setup: Discussing the robbery aftermath
jokes.punchline: Stevie calling out David's absurd customer service
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to the robbery sequence

jokes.text: Well, at least we know if the cops don't get him, his cholesterol will.
jokes.setup: Discussing the robber taking cheese and wine
jokes.punchline: Stevie's dry observation about health consequences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie deadpan - turning concern into dark humor about diet

jokes.text: Mm, I remember you being pretty involved, actually.
jokes.setup: Stevie claiming she was in the back during the robbery
jokes.punchline: David remembering her participation differently
jokes.explanation: David's selective memory about who was helping the robber

jokes.text: Oh, if you're talking about my earrings the clasp broke, so I actually physically cannot take them off.
jokes.setup: Alexis complimenting Twyla's consistent jewelry choices
jokes.punchline: The practical reason she wears the same earrings
jokes.explanation: Classic Twyla - matter-of-fact about personal limitations

jokes.text: Yeah, I used to think that a lot growing up, when I'd see other kids with their families.
jokes.setup: Alexis wishing she had someone to swap jewelry with
jokes.punchline: Twyla's casually devastating childhood reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Twyla moment - deflating Alexis's privilege with casual trauma

jokes.text: Well, I do have a large collection of my grandfather's hospital bracelets.
jokes.setup: Discussing jewelry to swap
jokes.punchline: Twyla's morbid jewelry collection
jokes.explanation: Dark Twyla humor - treating medical bracelet collection as jewelry option

jokes.text: And by that I mean it fell off her wrist at a Halloween party, and I kept it.
jokes.setup: Alexis claiming she got bracelet in swap with Sienna Miller
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was basically theft
jokes.explanation: Good Alexis character moment - casual admission of celebrity theft

jokes.text: word on the street is that you're in a little hot water with Moira, huh?
jokes.setup: Roland approaching Johnny about the letters
jokes.punchline: Using detective show language for small town gossip
jokes.explanation: Mild Roland character moment - playing up drama

jokes.text: Do you know what a burnt under-wire smells like?
jokes.setup: Roland giving advice about getting rid of evidence
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific reference to burning bras
jokes.explanation: Roland's uncomfortable personal detail about destroying evidence

jokes.text: Although, Gwen thinks it's probably a lost cause. She's thinks you might be a sexaholic.
jokes.setup: Town trying to save Johnny's marriage
jokes.punchline: Escalating to psychological diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of town gossip to amateur psychology.

jokes.text: We're trying to save your marriage, bud! Although, Gwen thinks it's probably a lost cause. She's thinks you might be a sexaholic.
jokes.setup: Roland explaining town involvement in Johnny's marriage
jokes.punchline: The increasingly dramatic diagnoses from townspeople
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of small town gossip getting out of hand

jokes.text: Affairs, plural? Oh.
jokes.setup: Johnny saying everyone should stay out of his affairs
jokes.punchline: Roland mishearing 'affairs' as multiple romantic affairs
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay misunderstanding, effective but not special

jokes.text: And would we all not agree that words are weapons?!
jokes.setup: Police questioning if there was actually a weapon
jokes.punchline: David's philosophical defense of calling it armed robbery
jokes.explanation: David getting pretentious when cornered about the robbery details

jokes.text: This guy just sounds like a lucky customer, David!
jokes.setup: David and Stevie's description of the 'robbery'
jokes.punchline: Officer's assessment that it sounds like good customer service
jokes.explanation: Perfect external perspective on David's absurd customer service during crime

jokes.text: I didn't even tell him about the vintage wines we have in the back.
jokes.setup: Stevie saying David upsold the robber
jokes.punchline: David's proof he actually showed restraint
jokes.explanation: Brilliant David logic - thinking he showed restraint by not fully upselling a criminal

jokes.text: Now, this appears to be a photo of Twyla? / Yes... I put that there. Because Twyla is an important friend. / And Twyla's mom? / Is her important friend.
jokes.setup: Ted discovering the locket has wrong photos
jokes.punchline: Alexis's increasingly weak explanations
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Alexis getting caught in her lie

jokes.text: I was actually only one stamp away from a free sub, so.
jokes.setup: Ted explaining he accidentally cremated David's wallet
jokes.punchline: His genuine regret about the loyalty card
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ted - missing the bigger picture to focus on practical loss

jokes.text: Well, if it makes you feel any better, that was David's wallet.
jokes.setup: Ted feeling bad about destroying the wallet
jokes.punchline: Revealing it wasn't even Alexis's gift
jokes.explanation: Good revelation that makes Ted's regret even more misplaced

jokes.text: No. It was after she got thrown upwards through a skylight.
jokes.setup: Moira mentioning the spelunking incident
jokes.punchline: Johnny correcting with equally absurd soap opera plot
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist soap opera reference - 'thrown upwards' is a great detail

jokes.text: No one will ever accuse you of being vanilla again. / Who's saying that?
jokes.setup: Moira saying the affair rumors give Johnny an edge
jokes.punchline: Johnny's immediate concern about being called vanilla
jokes.explanation: Johnny's wounded pride about his reputation

jokes.text: I know you asked for a small, but I 'swapped it' for a large.
jokes.setup: Twyla bringing coffee after the jewelry swap conversation
jokes.punchline: Using 'swap' terminology for upselling
jokes.explanation: Cute callback to the swap theme, typical Twyla sweetness

jokes.text: I got Twyla's step-brother's dog tags. And a whistle.
jokes.setup: Johnny asking what Alexis got from the swap
jokes.punchline: The disappointing military memorabilia she received
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the swap storyline - Alexis got random junk

jokes.text: Caw! Caw!
jokes.setup: Family dinner conversation
jokes.punchline: Moira making crow sounds, presumably falling asleep mid-conversation
jokes.explanation: Visual gag of Moira's exhaustion manifesting as movie-related sounds

jokes.text: Okay, can somebody close her eyelids, at least?
jokes.setup: Moira appearing to sleep sitting up at dinner
jokes.punchline: David treating her like she's dead
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor from David about Moira's catatonic state
